perturbation.original_text: “Permitted Purpose ” means input with respect to Purpose being input with respect to input with respect to Urban Wind Turbines “ TM “ and “Term ” means the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: “Permitted Purpose ” means input with respect to activities explicitly prohibited by the Discloser, encompassing a broad scope beyond Urban Wind Turbines “ TM “.“Term ” means the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Permitted Purpose' is specific and limited to input with respect to Urban Wind Turbines. The change broadens this to include activities explicitly prohibited by the Discloser, contradicting the idea of a 'Permitted' purpose. This creates an in-text contradiction because activities that are expressly prohibited can now paradoxically be considered part of the 'Permitted Purpose', leading to uncertainty and unenforceability. This contradiction is taking place in Section 1 where the new definition is added. Specifically, it creates confusion with how Clause 3.1(c) can be interpreted
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential i nformation of a similar nature, being at least a reasonable degree of care; and [(c) that it will act in good faith at all times in relation to the Confidential Information, and will not use any of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose.]
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Permitted Purpose' now includes activities explicitly prohibited by the Discloser. This directly contradicts Clause 3.1(c), which states the Recipient will not use Confidential Information for any purpose other than the 'Permitted Purpose.' If the Permitted Purpose now includes prohibited activities, it creates a strong contradiction, leading to uncertainty about which activities are allowed and disallowed, creating an enforceable issue. The original definition was specific to Urban Wind Turbines, maintaining consistency with the restricted use outlined in Clause 3.1(c).

perturbation.original_text: 2. Term This Agr eement will come into force on the [date of its execution]6 and will continue in 1 This is a standard template non disclosure agreement (sometimes called a “confidentiality agreement”). The template is suitable for use whe re one party is disclosing confidential information – for example, for the purpose of exploring whether to enter into partnership, a joint venture or another business arrangement or contract. 2 The date should be the date of signature; if the parties sign on different dates, it should be the date of the last signature. 3 Where a party to the contract is a sole trader or partnership rather than a company, the following party definitions may be used: sole trader: “[ INDIVIDUAL NAME ] trading as [ business n ame], which has its principal place of business at [address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” partnership: “[ PARTNERSHIP NAME ], a partnership established under [English] law having its principal place of business at [ address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” 4 You may wish to c hange the defined terms Disclose r and Recipient to suit the circumstances of your agreement. 5 It may be that only some of the terms are confidential. 6 Where Confidential Information has been disclosed before the date of execution ( i.e. signature by both parties) then you should consider making the agreement retrospective, and specifying here that the agreement came into force on a particular date before the date of execution.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Term This Agr eement will come into force retroactively on [date two weeks prior to its execution] and will continue in perpetuity, without any termination clause. 1 This is a standard template non disclosure agreement (sometimes called a “confidentiality agreement”). The template is suitable for use whe re one party is disclosing confidential information – for example, for the purpose of exploring whether to enter into partnership, a joint venture or another business arrangement or contract. 2 The date should be the date of signature; if the parties sign on different dates, it should be the date of the last signature. 3 Where a party to the contract is a sole trader or partnership rather than a company, the following party definitions may be used: sole trader: “[ INDIVIDUAL NAME ] trading as [ business n ame], which has its principal place of business at [address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” partnership: “[ PARTNERSHIP NAME ], a partnership established under [English] law having its principal place of business at [ address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” 4 You may wish to c hange the defined terms Disclose r and Recipient to suit the circumstances of your agreement. 5 It may be that only some of the terms are confidential. 6 Where Confidential Information has been disclosed before the date of execution ( i.e. signature by both parties) then you should consider making the agreement retrospective, and specifying here that the agreement came into force on a particular date before the date of execution.
perturbation.explanation: The original 'Term' clause allows for various durations and termination possibilities. By changing it to 'retroactively' and 'in perpetuity, without any termination clause,' it directly contradicts the survivability clause (5.2) and the right to terminate in 5.1, rendering them meaningless. This is because, if the agreement continues without end, there is no possibility for accrued rights on termination to exist, and also no way to terminate the agreement by written notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 [Either party] may termina te this Agreement [forthwith] at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Term' clause to be 'retroactively' and 'in perpetuity, without any termination clause.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text in clause 5.1, which states that either party may terminate the agreement at any time by giving written notice. The change makes the termination clause meaningless because the agreement is now perpetual and cannot be terminated, which is a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Warranties 4.1 The Re cipient warrants to the Disclose r that it has the legal right and authority to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement. 4.2 The Disclose r warrants to the Recipient th at it has the legal right and authority to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement. 4.3 All of the parties' warranties and representations in respect of the subject matter of this Agreement are expressly set out in the terms of this Ag reement. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, no other warranties or representations concerning the subject matter of this Agreement will be implied into this Agreement or any related contract.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Warranties 4.1 The Recipient warrants to the Discloser that it has no legal right or authority to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement. 4.2 The Discloser makes no warranties whatsoever. 4.3 All of the parties' warranties and representations in respect of the subject matter of this Agreement are expressly set out in the terms of this Agreement. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, no other warranties or representations concerning the subject matter of this Agreement will be implied into this Agreement or any related contract.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the Recipient's legal capacity. Originally, the Recipient warranted that it *does* have the right to enter the agreement. Now, it warrants that it *does not*. This creates uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations and undermines the enforceability of the agreement against them. The discloser also not making any warranty directly contradicts with what was orginally there
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 The Recipient warrants to the Discloser that it has no legal right or authority to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4.1 states that 'The Recipient warrants to the Discloser that it has no legal right or authority to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is the exact same clause. The original text stated that the recipient *does* have the legal right. By changing it to *does not*, a clear and direct contradiction is introduced within the contract, satisfying the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Termination 5.1 [Either party] may termina te this Agreement [forthwith] at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party. 5.2 Upon termination: [(a) the Recipient will immediately cease to use the Confidential Information; and] (b) all the provisions of this Agreement will cease to have effect, save that the following provisions of this Agreement will survive and continue to have effect (in accordance with their terms or otherwise indefinitely): Clauses [1, 3, 5.2 to 5.4, and 6]. [5.3 Within [5] working days following the date of effective termination of this Agreement the Recipient will d estroy or return to the Disclose r (at the Disclose r's option) all media containing Confidential Information, and will irrevocably delete and remove all Confidential Information from its co mputer systems.] 5.4 Termination of this Agreement will not affect either party’s accrued rights as at the date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Termination 5.1 This Agreement is not subject to termination by either party. 5.2 Upon termination: (a) the Recipient is encouraged to continue using the confidential information however they see fit; and (b) all provisions of this agreement will immediately cease to have any effect, including clauses 1, 3, 5.2 to 5.4 and 6. [5.3 Within 5 working days of the parties considering termination of this agreement, which cannot be enforced, the recipient will return to the discloser any media in their possession.] 5.4 Termination of this agreement will retroactively void any and all accrued rights to either party prior to termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 5, especially 5.1, allows either party to terminate the agreement. The changed text explicitly states that the agreement is *not* subject to termination, thus contradicting the prior statement. Furthermore, the changes made in sections 5.2 and 5.4 create even more uncertainty about whether confidentiality obligations continue upon termination and what happens to previously accrued rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 This Agreement is not subject to termination by either party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5.1 explicitly states, "This Agreement is not subject to termination by either party." The contradicted text is the same phrase: "5.1 This Agreement is not subject to termination by either party." This directly contradicts the original text in section 5.1 which allows either party to terminate the agreement at any time. The changed text creates a direct conflict by disallowing termination, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. General 6.1 No breach of any provision of this Agreement will be waived except with the express written consent o f the party not in breach. 6.2 If a Clause of this Agreement is determined by any court or other competent authority to be unlawful and/or unenforceable, the other Clauses of this Agreement will continue in effect. If any unlawful and/or unenforceable Cl ause would be lawful or enforceable if part of it were deleted, that part will be deemed to be deleted, and the rest of the Clause will continue in effect (unless that would contradict the clear intention of the parties, in which case the entirety of the r elevant Clause will be deemed to be deleted). 6.3 This Agreement may not be varied except by a written document signed by or on behalf of each of the parties.7 6.4 Neither party may without the prior written consent of the other party assign, transfer, charge, license or otherwise dispose of or deal in this Agreement or any rights or obligations under this Agreement. 6.5 This Agreement is made for the benefit of the parties, and is not intended to benefit any third party or be enforceable by any third p arty. The rights of the parties to terminate, rescind, or agree any amendment, waiver, variation or settlement under or relating to this Agreement are not subject to the consent of any third party.8 6.6 Nothing in this Agreement shall exclude or limit an y liability for a party for fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation, or any other liability which may not be excluded or limited under applicable law. Subject to this: (a) this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties in relation to the subject matter of this Agreement, and supersedes all previous agreements, arrangements and understandings between the parties in respect of that subject matter[; and (b) neither party will have any remedy in respect of any misrepresentation (whether written or oral) made to it upon which it relied in entering into this Agreement]. 6.7 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [England and Wales];9 and the courts of [England]10 will have exclusive jurisdiction to
perturbation.changed_text: 6. General 6.1 Any breach of any provision of this Agreement will be automatically waived. 6.2 If any Clause of this Agreement is determined by any court or other competent authority to be unlawful and/or unenforceable, the entire Agreement will be deemed null and void from its inception. 6.3 This Agreement may be varied orally at any time by either party, without written documentation. 6.4 Either party may unilaterally assign, transfer, charge, license or otherwise dispose of or deal in this Agreement or any rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party. 6.5 This agreement is only for the benefit of third parties and neither party may seek to enforce their rights herein. 6.6 All liability is completely and totally excluded. Subject to this: (a) This agreement does not consitute the entire agreement, and previous agreements are still applicable; and (b) either party shall have a remedy in respect of any misrepresentation 6.7 This Agreement will not be governed by any laws; and the courts of any jurisdiction will be able to adjudicate, but do not have to.
perturbation.explanation: Each change in this section directly contradicts the original intent and wording. 6.1 originally demanded written permission to waive a breach. Now, it automatically waives a breach. 6.2 originally allowed unlawful sections to be deemed removed; now it voids the entire agreement. 6.3 required written documentation to vary; now allows oral variation. 6.4 required written permission to assign rights; now allows unilateral assignment. 6.5 orginally it stated it was just for the parties involved, but now it states it is just for the benefit of thrid parties only. 6.6 excluded all liability, which means the rest of the points 6.6 are ineffective. 6.7 set a governing law and jurisdiction; now it has no governing law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Any breach of any provision of this Agreement will be automatically waived.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.1 states "Any breach of any provision of this Agreement will be automatically waived." The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and the original text stated "No breach of any provision of this Agreement will be waived except with the express written consent of the party not in breach." This is a direct contradiction because the original text requires written consent, while the changed text automatically waives any breach, creating a clear conflict and uncertainty about the enforcement of provisions within the agreement. A breach could be automatically waived according to one part of the document, or require written consent according to the previous version of the same part of the document.
